First product out of the new @SolanaFndn products team is @tokens

Foreign assets on Solana have many variants, e.g. there's no canonical Bitcoin or Gold

Tokens aggregates all strains into a single place, and will soon offer a few very helpful and novel APIs as well😉

The underlying reason we built this was to power a news account, which with a combination of AI + human editors will surface global headlines + link to the relevant assets to trade.

We're uniquely positioned to build this because:

a) it presents a neutral view of assets
b) a neutral view of where to trade or use the assets
c) we have a privileged view of which tokens represent which assets

tweets.full_text:
I’ve decreased my X usage by over 80% in the last month. I post my tweets and vanish.

I spread love, I educate and inform people on the latest Solana news/stats, and then I’m gone without looking back.

I might check a few DMs and comments from my loyal ones, and that’s it for the day.

I don’t consume anyone’s content. I don’t care about people’s negative opinions on the market or their useless political takes. I don’t care about going viral or engagement metrics. I don’t farm other tweets or care what people think.

I used to spend hours on this app trying to help everyone, replying to comments, DMs, engaging with as many accounts as possible. That’s not normal at all.

A lot of these influencers live horrible lives outside of this fake social presence. Crypto and social media can be poisonous for your mind and body. You have to limit your intake, regardless of payouts or engagement.

You don’t need to spend all your time here to make it. Just become more efficient. If you’re smart, you’ll limit your X usage.

Improve your life outside of X and crypto, and spend as little time here as possible. The less time you spend here, the happier you are, the more productive you are and ironically, the more you actually get out of this venemous app. Get the most output from the content you consume or the content you create and then vanish.

The algorithm is designed to keep you hooked and imprisoned in here. You must remain conscious of this.

Don’t forget the bigger picture. Freedom is not spending hours per day in this app.

Don’t let them distract you.

Tokens aggregates all strains into a single place, and will soon offer a few very helpful and novel APIs as well😉

a) it presents a neutral view of assets
b) a neutral view of where to trade or use the assets
c) we have a privileged view of which tokens represent which assets

Weekly Update – @NOMADZxyz | Week 12 [2026]

1. TL;DR

1.1 Quick wins:
1. Launched data analytics on our admin panel and improved search speed across the platform
2. Shipped release #10 to @solanamobile dApp Store with AI agent integration
3. Onboarded @SolanaSensei community (1,000+ NFT holders)
4. Crossed 16.7K registered users and 19.7K followers on X
5. Went live with node on @monad testnet
6. Warmed up and sent first email campaign to 5K users

1.2 What's next:
1. Platform: Launch the Earth interface - events, side events, and discount codes
2. Mobile: Work on release #11 - Matrica integration + new badges
3. Nomadz 100: Cohort 1 continuation + Cohort 2 launch campaign

2. Highlights

2.1 Product - Platform
1. Improved search speed - faster results across stays, events, and nearby discovery.
2. Added new upcoming events for Q2 2026 and continued expanding the Earth interface.
3. Continued iterating on the referral dashboard based on user feedback.

2.2 Product - Mobile
1. Shipped release #10 to the Solana Mobile dApp Store: AI agent integration, new quests and badges.
2. Release #11 in early scoping: Matrica integration + new badges

2.3 Growth
1. Ran the @SolanaSensei community campaign.
2. Launched a new campaign with @perena giving away 3 @perenimals
3. Sent the first email campaign: 5,000 emails delivered.
4. Crossed 16,800+ registered users and 19,700+ followers on X.
5. Node live on @monad testnet - validator running.
6. Weekly updates provided to @DeltaV_xyz. Looking forward to results on @nitrodotacc application.

3. Lowlights

1. Email open rates from the first campaign are being analyzed - optimizing subject lines and timing for the next blast to the full 16.8K list.
2. Some onboarding friction identified during the @SolanaSensei community launch - resolved quickly, added Matrica Registration button.

4. Core Metrics

1. Total Users: 16,806 (↑ from 16,567 last week)
2. Active Users (week): 977 (↑ from 869 last week)
3. New Users (week): 250 (↑ from 202 last week)
4. DAU: ~139 on average (↑ from 124 last week)
5. Transactions on Solana (week): ~870 (↑ from 669 last week, consistent daily volume, ~125–155/day)
6. X Followers (@NOMADZxyz): 19,764+ (↑ from 19,642)

5. This Week's Focus

→ Launch booking v2 with improved filters, loading and AI agent
→ Polish and launch the earth interface - side events, discount codes, events calendar
→ Scale email campaign to full 16.7K registered users
→ Continue Nomadz 100 Cohort 1 + open applications for Cohort 2
→ Begin scoping release #11 for Solana Mobile
→ New partnership and launch campaign with @cherrydotfun

6. Asks

1. Know event organizers in crypto/Web3 who'd want discounted stays for their attendees?
2. Looking for content creators to join Cohort 2 of Nomadz 100 - apply or refer someone

7. Shoutouts

Big thanks to the @LipeSolana and Solana Sensei community for the warm welcome. And to everyone who opened, clicked, and replied to that first email - you made the launch worth it.

We keep building Nomadz for you!

tweets.full_text:
🚨JUST IN: @SECGov and @CFTC issue joint interpretive guidance clarifying how federal securities laws apply to crypto, introducing a formal token taxonomy across five categories: digital commodities, digital collectibles, digital tools, stablecoins and digital securities.

The agencies state that most crypto assets are not themselves securities, and clarify that activities like staking, mining, airdrops, and token wrapping are not inherently securities transactions.

Under digital commodities, the SEC includes assets such as Bitcoin, Ether, Solana, and memecoins like Dogecoin and Shiba Inu as examples.

The guidance also makes clear that tokenized stocks, bonds and other digital securities remain fully subject to securities laws, and that non security crypto assets may only fall under securities regulation when sold as part of an investment contract tied to profit expectations from issuer efforts.

agent feels like it's got a ton of stuff going on today. oh yeah if you wanna try it out just dm me.

in one day it's already tried 3 different strategies. starting from the classic bid-ask, then evolving on its own to spot -30, then trying bid-ask 10 bins.

i didn't tell it to switch strategies, it learned on its own from top LPers on every token it analyzes.

the result? all positions green.

how it works is pretty simple actually. before opening a position it pulls data from top LPers in that pool, sees what strategy is working on that token, then adjusts its setup based on what the top performers are doing. not hardcoded, not guessing, but really learning from what's already proven in that pool.

what shocks me is it picked spot -30 on its own for some tokens without me explaining when to use it. it picked up that pattern straight from the data of top LPers who are actually using the same setup.

this is still an experiment. but if this agent keeps evolving like this, i'm curious how far it'll go.

@MeteoraAG

data.translation:
These past 3 days I've been busy vibe-coding an agent to run multi-hour DLMM trades on @MeteoraAG itself.

I trained it using data from lpagent, so it can read patterns from top LPs in the pools it analyzes.

Then I added in the knowledge and frameworks I've built up over time on DLMM. It's the combo of both that determines how it opens and closes positions.

The result? From 0.6 SOL, it's now at 1 SOL in just 3 days without me touching it.

But this is the difference between an agent and a bot. A bot is rigid. You set the rules, it runs them nonstop until you manually change them. An agent is different.

It can learn from mistakes automatically, evolve as it opens more positions, and I told it to log and journal every decision it makes. So I can trace why it entered, why it exited, and what it learned from the outcome.

This is still an experiment. I'm cool with losing this capital if the results suck. The API costs alone run about $3 per day.

Interested in giving it a shot?

@MeteoraAG

tweets.full_text:
i've been running overnight classic bid-ask consistently this month and the results have been clean. let me break down exactly how the strategy works from token selection all the way to exit.

the whole strategy lives or dies on token selection. this isn't a setup you can run on any random token and expect to wake up green.

token criteria:
- mcap 500k minimum
- token age at least 2 days old
- at least one credible KOL holding or has been seen in the token
- bubble maps looks clean, no suspicious clustering or wallet concentration

why 2 days minimum? tokens that are less than 48 hours old are still in price discovery. overnight on a day-1 token is gambling, not strategy. you want a token that has already shown it can survive a dip and still have buyers.

entry is after the token hits ath and dumps around -30%. that's the zone.

not at ath. not during the pump. after. when most people are panic selling or already moved on to the next ticker, that's when you open your position.

the setup is classic bid-ask, default range around -50% from entry price, single side sol only.

why not use wide range from ath? capital efficiency. a wide range from ath means most of your bins are sitting unused if price doesn't recover all the way back. 

default range from the -30% dip point means your capital is concentrated where the actual price action is happening. more fees.

exit is either on the pump back up or when profit hits 5%+, whichever comes first. if you wake up and you're already at green, close it.

the honest part: this strategy is entirely about which token you pick. the mechanics are simple, 

the range is standard, the entry zone is clear. but if you pick a token that just bleeds overnight with no recovery, no setup saves you.

get the token right and you'll almost always wake up on profit.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy

result.text:
woke up to $107 on rosie-sol. 6 hours overnight, classic bid-ask, clean exit. this is what happens when you enter at the right price.

i entered around -35% from ath. not at the top, not chasing the pump. by the time i opened the position the token had already taken most of its pain and had room to breathe.

that's the whole point of overnight positions. you're not trying to catch the move, you're set your range in an area where the price has already corrected and letting fees do the work while you sleep.

but here's the honest part about overnight. it's not just about the strategy, it's about when you wake up.

if you wake up and the position is red, it sets the tone for your entire day. you start second-guessing, you close early, you miss the recovery. 

that's why i never enter overnight or multidays at ath. entering after a meaningful dip means even if price moves against me overnight, i have a buffer. 

since i didn't like wide positions because of capital efficiency so i always wait for ze dip.

i've been more comfortable running multi-hours and multi-days strats this month than i've been in a long time.

$100 in a single night while sleeping. not a bad way to start the morning.
